payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Child earnings record aggregated by time period
Child performance report containing completion metrics and compensation data
Task claiming and completion tracking process
Task assignment and compensation definition process
Compensation distribution process based on performance verification
Provide children transparency into task opportunities and accomplishments
Motivate children through visibility of earnings and financial progress
Enable monetary reward tracking: Support the business objective of tracking children's earned compensation through completed task management.
Support payment fulfillment: Enable parents to accurately identify and execute payment obligations to children based on completed work.
iOS platform deployment node specification for mobile operational execution.
Android platform deployment node specification for mobile operational execution.
iOS platform deployment node specification for mobile operational execution.
Windows platform deployment node specification for desktop operational execution.
End-of-week event triggering earned amount total availability for child actor interface access.
Weekly task reset event triggering availability refresh for claiming operations.
Temporal access constraint limiting child actor earnings visibility to weekly cycle boundary completion.
Temporal claiming constraint limiting task availability access to weekly cycle reset boundaries.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task assignment, claiming, completion tracking, and compensation distribution process based on performance verification
payload.justification: Claiming and tracking completed tasks represents a strategic business process the enterprise performs | Defining compensated task assignments with monetary values is a strategic process parents perform to establish the system framework | Accessing performance reports for accurate compensation distribution represents a strategic business process ensuring fair payment

payload.description: Task assignment with completion status and monetary value
payload.justification: Tasks are the core entity referenced across multiple signals as things that are assigned, valued, completed, and tracked - strategically important to the enterprise

payload.description: Child performance report aggregated by time period, containing completion metrics and compensation data
payload.justification: Weekly earnings summary represents a distinct entity tracking financial outcomes - strategically important for the compensation system | Performance reporting is identified as a distinct entity parents access for compensation decisions - strategically important for system governance

payload.description: Historical task record supporting longitudinal analysis
payload.justification: Task history is a distinct entity enabling trend analysis and accountability - strategically important for tracking and improvement

payload.description: Weekly task-earnings cycle for claiming, completion, and compensation
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly periodicity for task tracking and earnings summary - representing a strategic temporal cycle that structures system operations

payload.description: Children acting as task performers and earnings recipients
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference children as active participants who claim tasks, view inventory, and monitor earnings - a strategic stakeholder role

payload.description: Parents acting as task definers, compensation authorities, and performance reviewers
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as those who define tasks, access performance reports, and distribute compensation - a distinct strategic role with authority

payload.description: Enable children to manage and track completion of assigned household tasks for weekly monetary compensation
payload.justification: SG049 expresses fundamental purpose of the system - enabling task claiming and tracking for pocket money, which is a strategic goal at the Planner level

payload.description: Empower parents to establish and manage compensated task assignment framework with monetary valuation
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation to define task-payment structures, representing a strategic goal for parental control and family economic education

payload.description: Provide children transparency into task opportunities, accomplishments, and financial progress to motivate through visibility of earnings
payload.justification: SG051 captures the purpose of visibility, which serves the strategic goal of transparency and child engagement in the task system | SG052 identifies motivational purpose of earnings visibility, representing a strategic goal of incentivizing participation and teaching financial awareness

payload.description: Enable parents to access performance data for accurate and fair compensation decisions
payload.justification: SG053 establishes need for reporting access to support payment accuracy, a strategic goal ensuring fairness and proper system operation

payload.description: Support accountability and performance trend analysis through historical task data access
payload.justification: SG054 indicates requirement for longitudinal data, representing strategic goal of enabling retrospective analysis and accountability mechanisms

payload.description: Task claiming: The business process by which children select and associate themselves with available tasks for completion.
payload.justification: Claiming is identified as a core operational process within the business concept for children to acquire tasks.

payload.description: Task specification: The business process through which parents define new task entities with associated monetary values.
payload.justification: Parent capability to specify tasks represents a fundamental process for populating the task inventory.

payload.description: Task visibility provisioning: The capability ensuring children can view both available and completed tasks within relevant time periods.
payload.justification: Visibility requirements define a functional capability supporting child interaction with task data.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation: The capability to calculate and present cumulative monetary totals for child actors within weekly boundaries.
payload.justification: Aggregation represents a computational function generating derived financial reporting data.

payload.description: Parent reporting: The capability to display child-task-earnings associations for payment decision support.
payload.justification: Reporting is defined as a distinct functional capability supporting parent fulfillment processes.

payload.description: Weekly task reset: Available tasks are refreshed and reset at weekly temporal boundaries to repopulate the task inventory.
payload.justification: The cyclical reset is explicitly defined as a business rule governing task availability lifecycle.

payload.description: Historical data retention: Task entities and relationships must be preserved and remain accessible beyond their active operational period.
payload.justification: Retention mandates represent a business rule governing data lifecycle management.

payload.description: Task: A discrete unit of work assignable within the household system, characterized by completion status and associated monetary value.
payload.justification: Task is the central business entity referenced across multiple signals describing its attributes (monetary value), states (available, completed), and lifecycle (reset cycles).

payload.description: Child: A participant entity within the household system who claims and completes tasks to accumulate monetary earnings.
payload.justification: Child is identified as a primary business entity with relationships to tasks and earnings data, distinct from the actor role.

payload.description: Parent: A participant entity responsible for defining tasks, monitoring child-task associations, and fulfilling payment obligations.
payload.justification: Parent is identified as a business entity with distinct capabilities for task creation and reporting, separate from the actor role.

payload.description: Monetary value: A financial amount attribute assigned to each task entity, representing the earning potential upon completion.
payload.justification: Monetary value is explicitly defined as an attribute associated with task entities for reward tracking purposes.

payload.description: Completion status: A state attribute of task entities indicating whether the task is available or has been completed.
payload.justification: Completion status distinguishes between available and completed task states, governing task lifecycle and visibility.

payload.description: Task-Child linkage: An associative relationship connecting completed task entities to the child entity who claimed them.
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines the linkage between completed tasks and child entities, forming a core business relationship.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total: An aggregated monetary attribute representing the sum of task values completed by a child within a weekly period.
payload.justification: This derived attribute aggregates individual task monetary values into a periodic total for business reporting.

payload.description: Historical task data: Archival records of task entities and their associated relationships maintained across temporal periods.
payload.justification: Historical data is treated as a distinct entity class requiring retention and accessibility across time boundaries.

payload.description: Weekly operational period: A recurring temporal cycle defining the boundaries for task availability, earnings aggregation, and task reset.
payload.justification: Weekly cycles are identified as the fundamental temporal unit governing multiple business operations.

payload.description: Task completion: A temporal event marking the transition of a task from available to completed status and triggering linkage to a child.
payload.justification: Completion represents a state-change event with business significance for tracking and earnings calculation.

payload.description: Weekly boundary: A temporal trigger point initiating task availability reset and potentially earnings reporting cycles.
payload.justification: Weekly boundaries explicitly trigger the cyclical business rule for task reset.

payload.description: Task claiming event: The temporal point at which a child associates with an available task, initiating the completion workflow.
payload.justification: Claiming represents a significant business event within the task lifecycle, though less explicitly detailed than completion.

payload.description: Child actor: A business participant who interacts with the system to view, claim, and complete tasks for monetary compensation.
payload.justification: Child is identified as an actor with specific interaction patterns and visibility rights within the business system.

payload.description: Parent actor: A business participant responsible for task creation, monitoring child performance, and authorizing payments.
payload.justification: Parent is defined as an actor with administrative capabilities and reporting access within the business context.

payload.description: Enable monetary reward tracking and payment fulfillment: Support the business objective of tracking children's earned compensation through completed task management and enable parents to accurately identify and execute payment obligations based on completed work.
payload.justification: Reward tracking is established as the core business concept and objective of the system. | Parent reporting capability explicitly supports the business goal of payment decision-making and fulfillment.

payload.description: Task visibility scope: Children's access to task data must be limited to temporally relevant available and completed tasks.
payload.justification: Visibility requirements impose a constraint on data access based on temporal and status boundaries.

payload.description: Historical data accessibility requirement: The system must maintain access to historical task records beyond their operational relevance period.
payload.justification: Retention mandate represents a constraint on data lifecycle management driven by business or compliance needs.

payload.description: Periodic task renewal: Task availability operates on recurring cycles to ensure continuous work opportunities for children.
payload.justification: The cyclical reset pattern reflects an underlying business principle of maintaining ongoing task availability rather than one-time assignments.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation process that computes total earned amounts from completed tasks for presentation to children.
payload.justification: Signal SG459 describes temporal aggregation logic as a computational process with defined periodicity and outputs.

payload.description: Task availability restoration process that resets task states to available status at the beginning of each weekly cycle.
payload.justification: Signal SG461 defines periodic state reset logic as an operational process restoring task availability.

payload.description: Aggregated reporting function providing consolidated views of child earnings and completion metrics to parent users.
payload.justification: Signal SG460 describes a logical reporting interface as a function delivering specific information products.

payload.description: Data visibility rule constraining child user access to only their assigned task collections.
payload.justification: Signal SG458 specifies data visibility requirements as normative access control constraints.

payload.description: Child user entity representing system participants who access and interact with task data.
payload.justification: Signal SG458 references 'child users' who require access to task collections, indicating a logical entity with distinct data visibility requirements.

payload.description: Parent user entity representing supervisory participants who monitor and review child activity data.
payload.justification: Signal SG460 identifies 'parents' as distinct actors requiring aggregated views, implying a logical entity with specific reporting relationships.

payload.description: Task entity representing discrete work units assigned to children with completion states and associated monetary values.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as central data objects with completion events, collections, and periodic availability states.

payload.description: Earnings entity representing accumulated monetary amounts derived from task completion activities.
payload.justification: Signals indicate 'earned amounts' as distinct data objects requiring temporal aggregation and reporting.

payload.description: Historical task record entity representing persisted task completion data over time.
payload.justification: Signal SG462 explicitly requires logical persistence of historical records as accessible data objects.

payload.description: Association between task completion events and child entities establishing ownership and accountability linkage.
payload.justification: Signal SG457 defines an explicit logical association rule linking completion events to child entities.

payload.description: Task completion state attribute indicating whether a task has been finished by a child user.
payload.justification: Signals reference completion as a state property of tasks that triggers associations and appears in reports.

payload.description: Task completion event occurring when a child finishes an assigned task, triggering association updates.
payload.justification: Signal SG457 explicitly references task completion events that activate association rules.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governing task availability, earnings computation, and state reset activities.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly periodicity as the fundamental temporal rhythm for task and earnings management.

payload.description: Historical data retention constraint requiring persistent storage and continued accessibility of past task records.
payload.justification: Signal SG462 mandates data persistence and accessibility as a normative design requirement constraining system behavior.

payload.description: Specific operating system versions designated as deployment targets for the platform.
payload.justification: Operating system versions represent physical nodes in the deployment infrastructure. At the Builder level, these are concrete technical platforms (e.g., Windows Server 2019, RHEL 8.5) rather than abstract location concepts.

payload.description: Technical requirement limiting deployment to designated operating system versions for platform compatibility assurance.
payload.justification: The normative signal explicitly describes a compatibility constraint that restricts deployment options. This represents a technical constraint at the Builder level governing which physical platforms may be used.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle boundary definition for earnings total access and task availability reset sequencing.
payload.justification: SG542 and SG544 both define weekly cycle boundaries—one for earnings access and one for task reset—representing the detailed temporal cycling specification for system operations.

payload.description: Weekly reset event triggering availability refresh of earned amounts for child actor claiming operations and interface access.
payload.justification: SG542 establishes a specific temporal boundary event when earned totals become accessible, representing a discrete timing event in the operational sequence. | SG544 specifies the timing when task availability resets, representing a discrete event that initiates a new operational claiming period.

payload.description: Multi-platform deployment specification encompassing iOS, Android, and Windows operating environments.
payload.justification: SG545 explicitly defines the deployment platform locations at the detailed specification level, identifying the three target operating system environments where the system must be implemented.

payload.description: iOS platform deployment node specification for mobile operational execution.
payload.justification: SG545 identifies iOS as a discrete deployment node representing a specific platform implementation target at the detailed network specification level. | SG545 identifies Android as a discrete deployment node representing a specific platform implementation target at the detailed network specification level. | SG545 identifies Windows as a discrete deployment node representing a specific platform implementation target at the detailed network specification level.

payload.description: Child user interface actor with task claiming execution capabilities and earnings visibility within weekly operational boundaries.
payload.justification: SG539, SG541, and SG542 collectively define the child actor's detailed operational interface capabilities including task claiming, viewing available/completed tasks, and accessing weekly earned totals. This synthesizes the child's complete user interface specification at the implementer level.

payload.description: Parent user interface actor with task administration and monetary assignment execution capabilities plus reporting access for distribution activities.
payload.justification: SG540 and SG543 together specify the parent actor's detailed operational interface for task configuration, value assignment, and report access functionalities required for pocket money distribution.

payload.description: Task administrator role specification responsible for task definition and monetary value configuration operations.
payload.justification: SG540 describes the operational role responsibilities of parents in configuring tasks and assigning monetary values, representing a distinct role specification at the implementer level.

payload.description: Task executor role specification responsible for claiming and completing task instances within defined temporal boundaries.
payload.justification: SG539 describes the operational role of children executing task claiming activities throughout the week, representing the task executor role at detailed specification level.

payload.description: Temporal constraint limiting access to child actor earnings visibility and task claiming to weekly cycle boundary completion/reset.
payload.justification: SG542 establishes a normative boundary constraint on when earnings can be accessed, representing a detailed validation rule that constrains operational behavior. | SG544 defines a normative timing constraint that governs when tasks become available for claiming, representing a detailed operational validation rule.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
